Returns a Body object.

Gets the silhouette edges for this face with the specified root point and in the specified direction. Root is an array of doubles defining the root point; Normal is an array of doubles defining the direction vector. The returned edges are transient, not added to the face, and cannot be found using IGetEdges or selected. The return array contains two elements for each edge: the first is the silhouette edge and the second is not used; iterate by stepping through every second element. Before calling, use IGetSilhoutteEdgeCount to determine the required array size.
Material_values must be an array of doubles of size 9.

The MacroMethods, ParamNames, and ParamValues parameters should be passed as string arrays. The ParamTypes parameter should be passed as an integer array. The non-i version automatically determines the parameter count from the provided arrays, whereas the i-prefixed version requires an explicit ParamCount parameter.

The object parameters should be arrays: MacroMethods is a string array, ParamNames is a string array, ParamTypes is an int array, ParamValues is a string array, DimTypes is an int array, DimValues is a double array, and IconFiles is a string array. The i-prefixed version uses explicit count parameters (ParamCount, DimCount, IconCount) but these are not needed when calling the non-i version as the array lengths are automatically determined.
The returned array contains Face2 objects. To determine the size of the array before calling this method, call IFeature::GetAffectedFaceCount.

The return array length divided by 3 gives the number of points (vertices) in the leader. Each point is represented by 3 consecutive doubles (x, y, z coordinates). The array contains 2 points for a straight leader, 3 points for a bent leader, or 4 points for a perpendicular leader.

The returned array includes all table annotations for this hole table feature, including any splits. Because many ITableAnnotation APIs apply to all parts of a split table annotation, you may only need to work with one annotation from the returned array. For example, changing the grid line thickness on one table annotation affects all splits of that table annotation. Call IHoleTable::GetTableAnnotationCount to get the total number of table annotations, including any splits, for this hole table feature.
The return value is an array of strings containing the names of the library feature configurations. Consider calling GetConfigurationCount first to determine the number of configurations.

Before calling this method, call GetSelectionCount to determine the size of the arrays. All output arrays (Objects, ObjectTypes, SelMarks, DrViews, ComponentXForms) are of size SelCount (the number of selected objects). ComponentXForms is not supported in VBA, VB.NET, C#, and C++/CLI; it is only available for in-process unmanaged C++.

The return value is specifically the neutral Face2 object. The i-prefixed version also returns the front face list, back face list, and their respective counts as separate out parameters, providing more structured access to the face data compared to the object array returned by the non-i version.
The return value is specifically a DisplayDimension object.

The returned System.object contains structured data including: the neutral face (Face2), separate lists of front faces and back faces, the count of front faces, the count of back faces, and the thickness. The i-prefixed method reveals these are exposed as distinct Face2 arrays with their sizes.

The Options parameter accepts BOOLEAN configuration options that can be combined. Available options include: swConfigOption_SuppressByDefault (true to suppress newly added features and mates in this configuration), swConfigOption_HideByDefault (true to hide newly added components), swConfigOption_MinFeatureManager (true to display only component names in the FeatureManager design tree rather than names and features), and swConfigOption_DontActivate (true to prevent the new configuration from being activated).
Returns a Face object. The EdgeInDisp parameter should be an Edge object.
The return value is a Face object. The out parameters FromFace1Disp and FromFace2Disp are also Face objects.
The return value is of type DisplayDimension (returned as System.object in the method signature).
The return value is specifically a DisplayDimension object, not just a generic object.
The return value is specifically a DisplayDimension object, not a generic object.
Creates a center line from P1 to P2.

The return value is an array of Face2 objects (not a single face) structured as described in Remarks: [Neutral face], [front face(s)], NULL separator, [back face(s)]. Cast the System.object return to an array to access individual Face2 elements in this arrangement.
The return value is specifically a SketchSegment object, not just a generic object.
The return value contains separate front face list and back face list arrays, each with an associated size. The method also returns a Face object and the Thickness parameter.
Return value is specifically a DisplayDimension object representing the newly created dimension.

Return type is SketchSegment (not a generic object).
Returns a FeatMgrView object (not a generic object).

Return type clarification: The method returns a SketchSegment object (not a generic object). Description: Creates a partial ellipse given a center point, two points that specify the major and minor axis, and two points that define the elliptical start and end points.
Returns a RefPlane object.

Return value is a RefPlane object.

Creates an elliptical arc trimmed between two points.

Return value is specifically an ISketchSegment object. The method creates lines in the currently active 2D or 3D sketch.

The return value is of type SketchSegment (not just System.object). You can use the returned object to call ISketchSegment APIs directly, or obtain the underlying ISketchSpline object by using QueryInterface.

The return type is RefPlane (not System.object as indicated in the signature). For the ProjOpt parameter: when true, projects the sketch plane point along the sketch plane normal for a sketch point and a surface; when false, projects the sketch plane point normal to the surface (rather than to the nearest location).

The ConfigNames parameter is an array. The I-prefixed version of this method requires an explicit Count parameter specifying the number of configurations, and ConfigNames is passed by reference as a string. In the non-i version, ConfigNames is passed as an object (typically an array) without requiring a separate count parameter.
The return value is a Feature object.

Return value is a splines enumeration (EnumSketchSegments type). The knot array within ParamsIn must contain exactly (Number of Control Points + Order) elements. When Dimension=4, each control point includes a fourth component w representing weight, formatted as (x, y, z, w).

The return value is specifically a Sketch object (returned as System.object). For an example of getting a sketch through feature traversal as an alternative approach, see the Get Sketches (C++) example.

The PropArray portion (dimension, order, number of control points, periodicity) consists of four integers placed in the first four doubles of the SafeArray for VBA compatibility.

Creates a spline on the active 2D sketch using the specified b-curve parameters. Returns true if created successfully, false if not. The spline must be G1 continuous; non-compliant data is rejected and no spline is created. Closed splines must be flagged as periodic. Coordinates are passed in sketch space with Z interpreted as 0.

The return value is an array of doubles (System.double), not System.object. In the derivative notation: dv represents the partial derivative in the v direction, N(u,v) represents the normal vector, and each P() entry represents a model space point or vector depending on its position in the array.
The BaseSurf out parameter is of type Surface, not a generic object.
The return value is specifically a Body object (returned as System.object in the non-i version).

The output arrays have specific structures: PointArray contains PointCount*3 values (three coordinates per point), TArray contains PointCount values (one parameter per point), and UvArray contains PointCount*2 values (two UV parameters per point).

The Radius, Location, and UVParameter output arrays each contain NumOfRadius elements. When NumOfRadius is 2, Radius contains two double values accessible as array indices 0 and 1, and Location and UVParameter each contain two IMathPoint objects at corresponding indices.
The return value is an array of doubles with 3 values per point (x, y, z coordinates), so the total number of doubles equals 3 times the number of points.
OtherCurve should be a Curve object. CurveBound is a double value. The output arrays PointArray, TArray, and UvArray contain double values.
The return value should be cast to Edge type.

Gets the b-spline surface parameters for the surface. The return value is an array of doubles containing surface parameters including knot vectors, control points, and surface orders. If you want to use the B-surface in combination with its trim curves, you should use IFace2::GetTrimCurves2 to extract both the trim curves and the B-surface together, as this provides better alignment between them since they are generated at the same time.
The TopDoc parameter should be of type ModelDoc2, and the Component parameter should be of type Component2, even though the non-i method signature accepts System.object for both.
BoundsArray contains 2 bound values for each curve in CurveArray (i.e., its size is twice the number of curves returned).
The TopDoc parameter is of type ModelDoc and the Component parameter is of type Component (not generic objects).
The PFilletItem parameter is expected to be a Vertex object.
Returns an Edge object.

The i-prefixed variant (IGetSplines3) is only supported in in-process, unmanaged C++ and returns a pointer to an array of doubles. It is not supported in VBA, VB.NET, C#, and C++/CLI. The non-i version (GetSplines3) should be used in managed environments.
The returned array contains Edge objects.
Return value is true if the entity was selected, false if not.

The method takes a count parameter of type System.int specifying the number of projection lines. Before calling this method, call GetProjectionLineCount to obtain the value for count. The return value is specifically an array of projection lines (in C++, a pointer to an array of projection lines), not just a generic System.object.

The Type field in the return value can be 0 (straight lines/polyline), 1 (arcs/circles), 2 (ellipses), or 3 (splines). For ellipses (Type=2), the GeomData array contains 17 doubles: center (3), unit vector along major axis (3), unit vector along minor axis (3), major radius (1), minor radius (1), startPt (3), endPt (3), and normal (3). For splines (Type=3), the GeomData size is calculated as (5 + numCtrlPts*numDims + numKnot) where numDims is 4 for rational splines or 3 for non-rational splines. The spline GeomData includes: degree, rational flag, numDims, numCtrlPts, numKnots, control point coordinates, and knot values. Although ellipses and splines are tessellated into polylines for rendering, their precise geometric parameters are also included in the GeomData array when Type is 2 or 3.
The return value is a WeldSymbol object, but it is returned as System.object and requires casting.
The rotationDir value in the returned array is specifically 1.0 for CCW (counterclockwise) and 0.0 for CW (clockwise), as the boolean is represented as a double.
